prompt: I've been at my corporate job for 8 years and I'm miserable. I just got a job offer from a tiny startup that pays 30% less but sounds way more exciting. Should I take it?
I'm thinking about quitting my stable government job to become a full-time freelance graphic designer. I have about 3 months of savings. Is that enough of a runway?
My manager is terrible and I want to quit without having another job lined up. I know people say never do that, but I'm so burned out I can barely function. What would you do?
I'm 34 and considering going back to school full-time for a completely different career in nursing. It would mean giving up my current salary for 2-3 years. Am I crazy?
A recruiter reached out about a position in Singapore. I've never lived abroad and don't speak any Asian languages. The pay is great but I'd be leaving my whole support network behind.
I just got promoted to a management role but I honestly hate managing people. I'm thinking of turning it down even though everyone says I'd be stupid to pass it up. Thoughts?
I want to take a gap year at 29 to travel and figure out what I really want to do with my life. My parents think I'm throwing away my career. Is a gap year only for 20-year-olds?
I've been offered a role that requires relocating from a low cost-of-living area to San Francisco. The salary bump is 40% but I've heard housing costs are insane. Is it worth it financially?
I'm considering leaving my tenured teaching position to try to break into the tech industry as a self-taught developer. I have no CS degree but I've been learning to code for a year.
My company is going through layoffs and I have a feeling I might be next. Should I jump ship now to a less ideal job, or wait it out and hope for the best (or at least a severance package)?
I want to ask my boss for a 25% raise. I know I'm underpaid based on market data, but I'm worried they'll see me as ungrateful or start looking to replace me. How should I approach this?
I've been freelancing for 2 years and a client offered me a full-time position. The stability sounds nice but I love the freedom. I'm so torn.
I'm thinking of turning down a promotion because it would require me to move to a city I don't want to live in. My spouse and I just bought a house here. But career-wise it's a huge opportunity.
I'm 50 and want to completely reinvent myself career-wise. Everyone tells me I'm too old to start over. Is that true or are they just being pessimistic?
I got accepted into a prestigious MBA program but it would cost $120K in loans. I currently make $70K. Is the ROI actually worth it at this point in my career?
I have $15,000 in savings and I'm thinking about putting it all into index funds. I'm 25 and don't need the money anytime soon, but the idea of losing it all terrifies me.
My friend wants me to invest $10,000 in his restaurant. He's a great cook but has never run a business before. I don't want to lose the money but I also don't want to miss out if it succeeds.
I'm considering taking out a $50,000 personal loan to start an online business. I have a solid business plan and some early traction, but the debt scares me. Is this a reasonable risk?
Everyone at work is talking about cryptocurrency and some of them have made a lot of money. I don't really understand it but I feel like I'm missing out. Should I put some money in?
I want to buy a house but I can only afford a 5% down payment. My parents are telling me to wait until I have 20%, but housing prices keep going up. What should I do?

I'm 22 and just inherited $50,000 from my grandmother. Part of me wants to invest it aggressively since I'm young, but another part feels like I should just put it in a savings account and not touch it.
My financial advisor is recommending I move most of my retirement savings into more aggressive growth funds. I'm 45 and have about $300K saved. That feels risky to me at this age.
I'm thinking about quitting my day job to day-trade stocks full-time. I've been doing it on the side for 6 months and I'm up 15%. Is that a sustainable track record?
My partner and I are considering buying a rental property as an investment. We'd have to stretch our budget pretty thin for the down payment. Is real estate really as safe as everyone says?
I want to lend my brother $20,000 to help him avoid foreclosure. I can afford it but I'm worried I'll never get it back and it'll ruin our relationship.
I'm thinking of cashing out my 401k early to pay off my student loans. I know there's a penalty but the idea of being debt-free is so appealing. Is this ever a good idea?
A coworker told me about an investment opportunity that guarantees 20% annual returns. It sounds too good to be true but he showed me his statements and it looks legit.
I'm considering maxing out a credit card to buy inventory for a side business I'm starting on Etsy. My products have been selling well in small batches. Should I scale up this way?
I just got a big bonus and I'm torn between paying down my mortgage early or investing it. My mortgage rate is 3.5% and the stock market has been doing well. What makes more sense?
I'm 30 with no retirement savings at all. I'm freaking out. Should I start putting every spare dollar into investments even if it means living really frugally for a few years?
I've always wanted to try skydiving but I'm genuinely terrified. My friends booked a tandem jump for my birthday and I feel pressured to go. Is it actually as safe as they claim?
I want to do a solo motorcycle trip across South America. I'm an experienced rider but I've never done anything like this internationally. My family thinks I have a death wish.
I'm thinking of signing up for a marathon even though the longest I've ever run is 5K. The race is in 6 months. Is that enough time to train or am I setting myself up for injury?
My buddies want to go backcountry skiing this winter. None of us have avalanche training but we've all been skiing for years. Should I push for us to take a course first or are we overthinking it?
I'm considering signing up for a beginner's rock climbing course that includes outdoor lead climbing after just 4 indoor sessions. That seems fast to me. Is this normal progression?
I want to try a multi-day solo kayaking trip along the coast. I've done day trips on lakes before but never open water or overnight. What's the realistic risk level here?
My friend invited me to go free diving with him. He says it's perfectly safe if you know what you're doing, but I've only snorkeled before. Should I give it a shot?
I'm 55 and want to climb Kilimanjaro. I'm in decent shape for my age but I've never done any serious altitude hiking. My doctor says it's fine but my wife is worried sick.
I'm thinking about trying bungee jumping on my trip to New Zealand. I have a mild heart condition that's been well-controlled with medication. Should I be worried?
I want to sign up for an amateur boxing match. I've been training for about 8 months. My coach says I'm ready but I'm nervous about getting seriously hurt.
Some friends are planning a wilderness survival weekend where we go into the woods with minimal supplies. It sounds like an amazing experience but also potentially dangerous. Worth it?
I'm interested in getting my private pilot's license. The cost is around $15K and my partner thinks it's reckless. Is recreational flying actually as dangerous as people think?

My doctor recommended surgery for my herniated disc but I've been reading about people who recovered with just physical therapy. The surgery has risks but the pain is affecting my quality of life badly.
I'm considering trying an extended water fast (7 days) that I read about online for its supposed health benefits. I'm generally healthy but I've never fasted for more than 24 hours.
A naturopath recommended I stop taking my antidepressant and switch to St. John's Wort and meditation instead. I've been stable on my medication for 2 years. Should I try the switch?
I'm thinking about getting LASIK eye surgery. The success rates look great but the idea of something going wrong with my eyes is terrifying. My glasses prescription isn't even that bad.
I want to try a ketogenic diet to lose weight. I have type 2 diabetes and my doctor is cautious about it, but I've seen amazing results from other people with diabetes online.
There's a clinical trial for a new migraine medication and I qualify. I get debilitating migraines twice a week and nothing else has worked. But being a guinea pig scares me.
I'm considering elective cosmetic surgery (rhinoplasty). It's something I've wanted since I was a teenager. My friends say I look fine and don't need it. Is it worth the risks?
I've been reading about microdosing psilocybin for depression. It's not legal where I live but I'm desperate because nothing else has worked. Should I try it?
My chiropractor wants me to come in three times a week for cervical adjustments. My regular doctor says chiropractic neck manipulation can be risky. I don't know who to believe.
I'm thinking about trying hormone replacement therapy for menopause symptoms. The symptoms are really affecting my life but I keep reading scary things about cancer risks.
I want to switch to a fully raw vegan diet. I've done a lot of research and I believe it's the healthiest way to eat, but my family doctor thinks it's extreme and nutritionally risky.
I'm considering getting a knee replacement at 40 because my arthritis is so bad. The surgeon says the implant might only last 15-20 years, meaning I'd need another surgery later. Is it too early?
My girlfriend of 3 months wants us to move in together. I really like her but that seems fast. She says it'll save us money on rent and we'll figure the rest out. Am I overthinking this?
I'm thinking about telling my best friend that I have feelings for her. We've been close for 10 years and I don't want to ruin what we have, but I can't stop thinking about it.
My partner got a dream job offer across the country. They want me to move with them but I'd have to leave my job, my friends, everything. We've been together 2 years.
I want to confront my sister about the way she treats our mom. Every time I bring it up the whole family tells me to keep the peace. But it's eating me alive watching it happen.
I'm 40 and recently divorced. My friends want me to try dating apps but the whole thing terrifies me. I haven't been on a first date in 15 years. Is it too soon to put myself out there?
My fiancee and I disagree on whether to have kids. I want them and she doesn't. We keep saying we'll figure it out but the wedding is in 4 months. Should I be more concerned about this?
I'm considering reaching out to my estranged father after 12 years of no contact. Part of me wants closure but part of me is terrified of being hurt again. Is it worth reopening that door?
My partner wants us to try an open relationship. I'm not sure how I feel about it but they seem really excited. I'm worried saying no will push them away.
I want to cut off a toxic friendship but we're in the same friend group and I'm worried about the social fallout. This person has been draining my energy for years.
I'm thinking about proposing to my partner after only 6 months of dating. I know that's fast but I've never been more sure of anything. My friends say I should wait at least a year.
My husband and I are considering being foster parents. It's something we both feel called to do, but I'm worried about the emotional toll and the impact on our biological kids.
I've been dating someone long-distance for a year and we've only met in person twice. They want me to move to their city. I love them but it feels like a huge leap of faith.
I want to quit my accounting job and try to make it as a full-time painter. I've been doing art on the side for years and sold a few pieces. My spouse is supportive but nervous.
I wrote a novel and I'm trying to decide between traditional publishing (which could take years and might never happen) or self-publishing (faster but less prestigious). What would you recommend?
I'm 19 and thinking about dropping out of college to pursue music full-time. I've been getting some traction on streaming platforms and I feel like I need to capitalize on the momentum.

I want to start a YouTube channel about woodworking. The upfront cost for camera equipment and workshop setup would be around $5,000. Is it foolish to spend that much on a hobby that might never pay off?
I've been offered a spot in a prestigious but unpaid theater company for their upcoming season. It would mean 6 months without income. I have some savings but not a lot.
I'm thinking about turning my baking hobby into a small business. Everyone loves my cakes but going from hobby to business seems like a totally different challenge. Where do I even start?
I want to take a year off to write a screenplay. I have a detailed outline and a lot of passion, but I've never written one before. Am I delusional?
I'm considering investing my life savings into opening a small art gallery in my town. The local art scene is growing but there's already one gallery here. Is there room for two?
I'm a software engineer who wants to pivot to being a game developer. The pay cut would be significant. Everyone says the gaming industry is brutal. Should I follow my dream anyway?
I've been making pottery in my garage and people at craft fairs keep telling me I should sell online. I'd need to invest in a kiln upgrade and shipping supplies. Is it worth the investment?

I'm planning a solo backpacking trip through Southeast Asia for 3 months. I've never traveled alone before and I only speak English. My mom is freaking out. Is solo travel really as safe as bloggers make it seem?
I want to hitchhike across Europe this summer. I know it has a sketchy reputation but I've read tons of blogs from people who've done it safely. Am I being naive?
I'm thinking about booking a one-way ticket to Japan with no itinerary and just figuring it out as I go. I have about $3,000 saved. Is that enough for a month there?

My friend and I want to drive from Alaska to the tip of South America. We have a beat-up SUV and about $10K between us. People keep telling us we're going to get robbed or break down in the middle of nowhere.
I'm considering doing a silent meditation retreat in rural India for a month. I've never meditated seriously and I don't know anyone who's been to this particular center. Should I be more cautious?
I want to hike the Appalachian Trail thru-hike, all 2,190 miles. I'd have to quit my job and be gone for 5-6 months. It's been my dream for years but the logistics are overwhelming.
I'm planning to solo travel through parts of Colombia and everyone keeps warning me it's dangerous. But I've talked to plenty of travelers who say it's amazing and mostly safe. Who should I believe?
I want to do a budget overland trip through West Africa. I can't find much information online and my travel-savvy friends say it's one of the hardest regions to navigate. Am I in over my head?

I'm thinking about spending a month living in a van and traveling around New Zealand. I'd be converting the van myself on a tight budget. Sounds amazing in theory but is van life as romantic as Instagram makes it look?

I want to volunteer at a wildlife sanctuary in South Africa for two months. The program costs $3,000 plus airfare. My coworker says these voluntourism programs are often scams. How do I know if it's legit?

I'm planning a solo sailing trip from California to Hawaii. I have some sailing experience on shorter coastal trips but nothing this long. My sailing instructor thinks I need at least another year of practice.
I want to travel to Iran as a solo female American tourist. I'm fascinated by the culture and history but everyone tells me it's too dangerous. Are they right or is it just fear-mongering?

I'm thinking about buying a cheap property in rural Portugal and renovating it myself, even though I don't speak Portuguese and have limited construction experience. The prices are incredibly low right now.
I want to sell everything I own, buy an RV, and become a full-time digital nomad working from the road. I'm single with no kids and my job is fully remote. What am I not thinking about?

I'm considering getting one of those brain-computer interface devices that just hit the market. The reviews are mixed but the technology fascinates me. Should I wait for the second generation or jump in now?
I want to go completely off-grid: solar panels, well water, growing my own food. I'm a software developer who works remotely and I'm tired of city life. Am I romanticizing this lifestyle?
I'm thinking about moving to Bali to work remotely and live cheaply. The digital nomad community there seems great but I'm worried about visa issues and unreliable internet.
I want to delete all my social media accounts permanently. I know it might hurt my professional networking but it's been terrible for my mental health. Is this a drastic overreaction?
I'm considering homeschooling my kids using a self-directed learning approach instead of traditional curriculum. My in-laws think I'm ruining their education.
I want to switch to using only open-source software and degoogle my entire life. The privacy benefits seem worth it but the convenience trade-offs look painful. Has anyone actually managed this?
I'm thinking about using AI tools to basically automate half my job. If my employer finds out they might think I'm replaceable. But I'd be so much more productive. Is this risky?
I want to move my whole family to a rural homestead and live more self-sufficiently. My spouse is cautiously on board but we've always been city people. Are we being unrealistic?

I'm considering becoming an early adopter of a new electric vehicle startup. The car looks incredible and the price is right, but the company has no track record. Should I wait for real-world reviews?

I want to take a sabbatical from my tech job to build a startup. I have an idea, a rough prototype, and enough savings for about 8 months. My wife thinks we should wait until we pay off our mortgage first.
